title: Quiz: Problem-Inspired Ansätze
description: Test your understanding of problem-inspired ansatz design

questions.question: What is the main advantage of problem-inspired ansätze over generic ones?

options.text: They constrain the search to physically meaningful states
They run faster on classical computers

questions.explanation: Problem-inspired ansätze encode domain knowledge to focus the search on states that are physically relevant, reducing the effective search space and often improving trainability.

questions.question: What does UCCSD stand for?

options.text: Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles

questions.explanation: UCCSD stands for Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles, a chemistry-inspired ansatz that includes single and double excitation operators.

questions.question: In QAOA, what is the role of the mixer Hamiltonian?

options.text: To enable exploration by creating superpositions

questions.explanation: The mixer Hamiltonian (typically ΣXᵢ) enables exploration of the solution space by creating superpositions, allowing the algorithm to escape local minima.

questions.question: The Hamiltonian Variational Ansatz (HVA) uses the terms of the problem Hamiltonian as circuit generators.
questions.explanation: True. HVA constructs the ansatz from exponentials of the Hamiltonian terms: |ψ(θ)⟩ = ∏ₖ e^{-iθₖHₖ} |ψ₀⟩, where H = Σₖ Hₖ.

questions.question: Which symmetry is most important for electronic structure calculations?
questions.explanation: Particle number conservation (fixed number of electrons) is essential for electronic structure. Violating it would give unphysical states with the wrong electron count.

questions.question: What type of gate preserves particle number in a fermionic system?
questions.explanation: Givens rotations mix |01⟩ ↔ |10⟩ states while preserving particle number. H and X gates can create/destroy particles, and T gate is a phase gate.

questions.question: For the MaxCut problem, QAOA's cost Hamiltonian typically contains which terms?

options.text: Two-qubit ZZ terms for each edge

questions.explanation: MaxCut's cost Hamiltonian has ZZ terms for each edge: H_C = Σ_(i,j)∈E ½(1 - ZᵢZⱼ). This equals 1 when qubits are in different states (edge cut).

questions.question: Which are advantages of the Hamiltonian Variational Ansatz? (Select all)

options.text: Always has fewer parameters than hardware-efficient

questions.explanation: HVA respects symmetries, has interpretable parameters (evolution times), and is connected to adiabatic computing. It doesn't always have fewer parameters—that depends on the Hamiltonian.
